question: When does the new authentication flow ship to production?

haystack_sessions.content: Quick check on the auth project — when are we cutting over?
The security team finalized the schedule yesterday. Production rollout is locked in for March 15, 2026, right after the penetration test signs off.
Great, I'll let the support team know.
Sounds good. I'll also update the rollout tracker so on-call knows.
What's the standup schedule for this week?
Standups are every weekday at 10am Pacific. Friday's slot is reserved for the retro instead.
Who is leading the dashboard rewrite?
The dashboard rewrite is owned by the platform team, with Priya as tech lead.
Did marketing approve the launch banner copy?
Yes, marketing signed off on the banner copy on Tuesday. The visual assets are still in review.

question: Which conference room did we book for the offsite kickoff?

haystack_sessions.content: Did facilities confirm a room for the offsite kickoff?
Yes, the kickoff is booked in Cypress Hall on the third floor. Capacity is 40, so we should be comfortable.
Perfect, I'll add it to the invite.
Where are we doing the engineering all-hands next month?
Engineering all-hands will be held in the main auditorium, same as last quarter.
Any update on parking validation for guests?
Reception now hands out one-day garage passes — no need to pre-register guests.
Is the catering order in for the QBR?
Catering for the QBR is confirmed with the usual vendor; sandwiches and salads for 60.

question: Across our planning chats, who ended up owning the migration runbook?

haystack_sessions.content: We need someone to drive the migration runbook end-to-end. Any volunteers?
Looking at recent capacity, the SRE team has the bandwidth. I can flag it to their lead in tomorrow's sync.
Update on runbook ownership?
SRE leadership reviewed the ask. They proposed Devika Rao as the directly responsible individual since she ran the last database cutover.
Sounds right, let's lock it in.
Confirmed. Devika Rao will own the migration runbook from start to finish.
Is the staging environment ready for the dry run?
Staging will be ready by end of week. The seed data refresh finishes Thursday.
What's the rollback budget if the migration goes sideways?
We have a 90-minute rollback window before customer-visible impact. Beyond that we trigger the incident bridge.

question: What did the team finally settle on for the project's external code name?

haystack_sessions.content: We need an external code name for the new analytics product. Any thoughts?
A few options on the table: Beacon, Lighthouse, Compass, and Pathfinder. Brand wants something nautical-adjacent.
Did the brand review come back on the name shortlist?
Brand narrowed it to Lighthouse and Compass. They flagged Beacon as too close to a competitor product.
Final decision on the code name?
Leadership signed off this morning. We're going with Project Lighthouse for all external communications.
I'll update the docs.
Reminder about the all-hands next week?
All-hands is Wednesday at 11am. Bring your own coffee — the espresso machine is still being serviced.

question: What is the latest official deploy frequency policy?

haystack_sessions.content: What's our deploy cadence again?
Right now production deploys go out once a week, on Wednesdays.
I heard the deploy policy changed — what's the current rule?
Yes, as of last sprint the policy was updated. Production deploys now happen twice per week, Tuesdays and Thursdays, to shrink batch sizes.
Got it, will update the team wiki.
Any change to the on-call rotation schedule?
On-call rotates weekly on Monday mornings. Shadow shifts are still optional.
Can we deploy on Fridays in an emergency?
Friday deploys require a VP approval and an active incident. Otherwise the freeze stands.

question: What's the current opening hours for the downtown branch after the recent change?

haystack_sessions.content: When is the downtown branch open?
Until last month, downtown was open from 10am to 6pm on weekdays.
Hours updated for downtown right?
Correct. Following the customer survey, downtown branch hours were extended to 9am to 7pm Monday through Friday, effective May 1.
Helpful, thanks.
What's the weekend coverage like?
Weekends remain unchanged: Saturday 10am to 4pm, Sunday closed.

question: How long had the reliability lead been at the company by the time of the architecture review?

haystack_sessions.content: When did Marcus first join the company?
Marcus, our reliability lead, started on April 3, 2018.
When was the architecture review held?
The architecture review took place in early April 2025, just before the spring planning summit.
Marcus mentioned a tenure milestone at the architecture review — how long had he been with us by then?
By the time of the architecture review, Marcus had been with the company for seven years. He referenced his anniversary in the opening remarks.
Nice, that lines up.
What was the main outcome of the architecture review?
The big outcome was a decision to consolidate the three regional data planes into a single unified control plane over the next two quarters.

question: How many sprints elapsed between the alpha demo and general availability of the search feature?

haystack_sessions.content: When did we first show off the new search internally?
The alpha demo of search was at the engineering all-hands on August 12, 2025.
When did search hit GA?
Search reached general availability on April 6, 2026, after the final round of perf tuning.
Roughly how many sprints did the search team take to get from alpha to GA?
Counting our two-week sprint cadence, the search team shipped GA eight sprints after the alpha demo. That includes the two-sprint scope freeze before launch.
Did the launch hit the marketing window?
Yes, launch landed within the marketing window with a few days of buffer.

question: Which teammate did our user say they pair best with on debugging sessions?

haystack_sessions.content: Honestly the person I pair best with for nasty debugging sessions is Hannah Kim — she keeps the hypotheses really tight.
Noted. Want me to default to suggesting Hannah Kim as a pairing partner when you flag debugging-heavy tickets?
Yes, please do that.
Will do. I'll keep that pairing preference on file.
Who runs the weekly architecture office hours?
Architecture office hours are hosted by Theo on Thursdays at 2pm.
Any feedback from the user research interviews this week?
Three interviews wrapped up. The headline finding was that onboarding still feels too text-heavy for new admins.

question: What was the final agreed retention period for raw application logs?

haystack_sessions.content: We need to lock in a retention policy for raw application logs. Compliance is asking.
Current default is 30 days. Compliance flagged that some audit workflows want longer retention, so we should revisit.
Where did the retention conversation land?
After the working session, we settled on 45 days for raw application logs. Aggregated metrics will keep their existing 13-month retention.
Will 45 days fit our storage budget?
Yes — finance reviewed the projected storage cost and signed off on the 45 days plan.
Any update on the SOC2 audit prep?
SOC2 prep is on track. The auditor visit is scheduled for late June.
Did we ever publish the data classification policy?
Yes, the data classification policy was published in early March and lives on the security wiki.
